from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about an artist into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple mapping from predicate to a readable clause template.
    # The placeholders {subject} and {object} will be filled in.
    templates = {
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": "{subject} is associated with {object}",
        "background": "{subject}'s background is {object}",
        "birthDate": "{subject} was born on {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "birthYear": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "genre": "{subject} performs {object} music",
        "instrument": "{subject} plays the {object}",
        "origin": "{subject} originates from {object}",
        "activeYearsStartYear": "{subject} started their career in {object}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": "{subject} ended their career in {object}",
        "occupation": "{subject} works as {object}",
        "recordLabel": "{subject} is signed to {object}",
        "deathPlace": "{subject} died in {object}",
        "deathDate": "{subject} died on {object}",
        "alternativeName": "{subject} is also known as {object}",
        "nationality": "{subject} is {object}",
        "professionalField": "{subject} works in the field of {object}",
        "musicFusionGenre": "{subject} fuses {object}",
        "musicSubgenre": "{subject} belongs to the subgenre {object}",
        "stylisticOrigin": "{subject} has stylistic origins in {object}",
        "language": "{subject} uses the {object} language",
        "officialLanguage": "{subject}'s official language is {object}",
        "derivative": "{subject} gave rise to {object}",
        "location": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "governingBody": "{subject} is governed by {object}",
        "leader": "{subject} is led by {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subject}'s leader holds the title {object}",
        "country": "{subject} is in the country {object}",
        "ethnicGroup": "{subject} belongs to the ethnic group {object}",
        "parentCompany": "{subject} is owned by {object}",
        "bandMember": "{subject} includes member {object}",
        "capital": "{subject}'s capital is {object}",
        "anthem": "{subject}'s anthem is {object}",
        "currency": "{subject}'s currency is {object}",
        "demonym": "People from {subject} are called {object}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "distributingCompany": "{subject} is distributed by {object}",
        "training": "{subject} was trained at {object}",
        "populationDensity": "{subject} has a population density of {object}",
        "meaning": "The meaning of {subject} is {object}",
        "longName": "{subject}'s long name is {object}",
        "areaTotal": "{subject} covers an area of {object}",
        "foundingDate": "{subject} was founded on {object}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{subject} is at an elevation of {object} meters",
        "postalCode": "{subject} has postal codes {object}",
    }

    # Build a list of clause strings for all triples
    clauses: list[str] = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate)
        obj = triple.object
        # Strip surrounding quotes if present
        if isinstance(obj, str) and ((obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'"))):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(subject=triple.subject, object=obj)
        else:
            clause = f"{triple.subject} {triple.predicate} {obj}"
        # Capitalize the first character of the clause
        clause = clause[0].upper() + clause[1:]
        # Remove any trailing period to avoid duplication
        clause = clause.rstrip('.')
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Post‑process to avoid repeating the same subject in consecutive clauses.
    # If a clause starts with the same subject as the previous one, replace it with "They".
    for i in range(1, len(clauses)):
        prev_subject = triples[i - 1].subject
        curr_subject = triples[i].subject
        if prev_subject == curr_subject:
            # Find the first space after the subject in the clause and replace the subject.
            # Assumes the clause begins with the subject followed by a space.
            space_idx = clauses[i].find(' ')
            if space_idx != -1:
                clauses[i] = "They" + clauses[i][space_idx:]

    # Join clauses into a single complex sentence.
    # Use semicolons for more than two clauses to improve readability.
    if not clauses:
        return ""
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0] + "."
    elif len(clauses) == 2:
        sentence = " and ".join(clauses) + "."
    else:
        sentence = "; ".join(clauses[:-1]) + "; and " + clauses[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
